evals.prompt: My prospect viewed the Qwilr proposal 4 times in the last 2 days, spending most of the time on the pricing section, but hasn't responded. It's a $75k deal and they mentioned they're also talking to a competitor.
evals.expected_output: Interpretation of the view pattern (heavy pricing attention = price comparison with competitor), urgency assessment, concrete action plan focused on reinforcing value vs. the competitor, and 1-2 follow-up message drafts that address price sensitivity without discounting preemptively.

assertions.name: has_signal_interpretation
assertions.description: Interprets the 4-view, pricing-focused pattern as price sensitivity or competitor comparison

assertions.name: has_action_plan
assertions.description: Provides concrete next steps addressing the competitive situation

assertions.name: has_follow_up_draft
assertions.description: Includes at least one drafted follow-up message

assertions.name: no_creepy_tracking
assertions.description: Follow-up messages don't explicitly reveal that view tracking is being used

evals.prompt: I sent a Qwilr proposal 5 days ago to Sarah Chen, VP of Operations at Pinnacle Logistics (a 300-person supply chain company). It's a $60k deal for our workflow automation platform. The demo last Tuesday went great — she said 'this is exactly what we need' and asked me to send pricing. I emailed the Qwilr link right after but she hasn't viewed it at all. I also sent a LinkedIn follow-up on day 3 with no response. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis of likely causes (email delivery issue, lost priority, busy period), escalating action plan starting with delivery verification and ending with alternative contact methods, and draft messages for re-engagement.

assertions.name: checks_delivery
assertions.description: First step is verifying the email actually landed (spam, correct address)

assertions.name: has_escalation_plan
assertions.description: Provides escalating steps from gentle to more direct outreach

assertions.name: has_alternative_channels
assertions.description: Suggests trying different contact methods beyond email

evals.prompt: Three different people at Cascade Corp viewed my Qwilr proposal this week — but I only sent it to my champion, Mike Torres, their Director of Operations. We're selling a $90k annual contract for our supply chain visibility platform and the deal is in the evaluation stage. Based on the email domains, the other two viewers look like they might be someone from finance and someone from IT. Mike hasn't told me he shared it. How should I handle this?
evals.expected_output: Interpretation that the proposal is being circulated to a buying committee (positive signal), advice on identifying who the additional viewers might be, and strategy for supporting the champion with materials for the broader group.

assertions.name: interprets_committee
assertions.description: Identifies multiple viewers as buying committee circulation — a positive signal

assertions.name: has_champion_strategy
assertions.description: Advises on how to support the original contact who is championing internally

assertions.name: suggests_stakeholder_content
assertions.description: Recommends creating stakeholder-specific materials for the additional viewers
